title: Brevet
content:
{{Top|leg}}
The commission of '''Brevet''' was accorded to [[Officer/Legends|officers]] who had been appointed to serve as a military officer in a—usually higher and often temporary—rank without a confirmed promotion to the specified rank.

==History==
On a mission to [[Haruun Kal/Legends|Haruun Kal]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] used his authority as a [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] to both swear [[Nick Rostu]] into the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] and award him a brevet rank of [[Major/Legends|major]]. In the aftermath of the [[Battle of Haruun Kal/Legends|Battle of Haruun Kal]], Windu recommended Rostu's rank be made permanent.

As a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], [[Myn Donos]] rose rapidly through [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] training and was given command of [[Talon Squadron (New Republic)|Talon Squadron]] upon graduation, with the brevet rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] over the [[training squadron]]. The rank was removed when he was reassigned after the loss of his [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] at the [[Ambush at Gravan Seven]].

The only Brevet Admiral specifically known by that title was [[Glie'oleg Kru]], who commanded the [[Bothan Assault Cruiser]] [[Champion (vehicle)|''Champion'']] until its destruction during the [[Battle of Kalarba]], early in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]].<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|118|''Champion''}}</ref> However, it appears that a similar position had also been used by the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], as [[Michael Unther]] had been the Admiral of a [[Sector fleet/Legends|Sector Fleet]] prior to his promotion to the rank of [[Rear Admiral/Legends|Rear Admiral]], which was almost certainly the lowest substantive flag rank in the hierarchy.

Under both the New Order and the New Republic, these brevet ranks were used when it was necessary to rapidly create new squadrons and local commands; under the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], such promotions may also have served to even out the inequalities within [[Sector fleet/Legends|Sector Group]] command structure, since it would have been somewhat absurd for every backwater [[Sector/Legends|sector]] with only a couple of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] to be accorded the same amount of senior Naval commanders as more significant Sectors where even third-tier subordinate commands were larger.
